title: Creamy Kenyan Foie Gras Terrine
description: A rich and creamy Kenyan dish featuring foie gras, perfect for a solo dinner, with a grassy and juicy flavor profile and no added sugar.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small saucepan, combine the chicken broth, kenyan tea leaves, and shallots, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, then strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
In a separate saucepan, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat, then add the foie gras and cook until browned on all sides.
Remove the foie gras from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
In a blender or food processor, combine the cooled foie gras, heavy cream, black pepper, and salt, and blend until smooth.
Pour the foie gras mixture into a terrine mold or a 9x5 inch loaf pan, and smooth the top.
Bake the terrine in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until set.
Remove the terrine from the oven and let it cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until chilled.
To serve, slice the terrine into thick slices and serve chilled.
